category_title: Students with ASD who struggle with emotional regulation and unstructured social interaction
minimal_context: Bruce is seated in a circle participating in a CoPI session where participants must agree or disagree with previous speakers' points.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Bruce, the next part of our CoPI session is to listen to the other children who are disagreeing with your point.

STUDENT: (Visibly frustrated) I don't want to listen to them.

TUTOR: It is time for them to share their thoughts.

STUDENT: I am angry because the other children were disagreeing with me.

TUTOR: Please stay in your seat in the circle while they finish speaking.

STUDENT: (Removes himself from the session)

options.A: Practice social skills in natural contexts when participants agree or disagree with previous speakers' points.
options.B: Refrain from sharing personal opinions, summarizing or interpreting student points, or providing a final conclusion to the discussion.
options.C: Use 'First-then' charts and token economy systems when participants agree or disagree with previous speakers' points.
options.D: Implement research-based instructional procedures (e.g., time delay, differential reinforcement, stimulus prompting) when participants agree or disagree with previous speakers.
